summary: Comprehensive end-to-end testing of Practora Phase 2 booking lifecycle. 20/20 backend pytest tests pass and all critical frontend flows verified via Playwright. Auth, policies, public booking creation, pro reschedule/cancel/no-show, customer magic-link access, slot conflict (409), schedule-outside (400), reschedule limit policy block, activity log immutability, and [EMAIL:CONSOLE] log emission all behave as specified.

frontend_flows_verified: Policies page: PUT persists and reloads correctly
Bookings list with All/Upcoming/Past/Cancelled filter pills clickable
Pro booking detail loads with status pill + activity timeline (5+ entries shown for booking with full lifecycle)
Pro reschedule dialog opens with calendar widget
Customer manage page (magic link) shows summary + status pill + reschedule/cancel buttons
Invalid token shows 'Link expired or invalid'
Customer reschedule view opens with date calendar
Customer cancel flow completes (shows All done success view)

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 852 lines - approaching the 700-line guideline; consider splitting into routers (auth, profile, bookings_pro, bookings_public, services, schedule) for maintainability.

_blocked_reason() in server.py is reasonable but message ordering could surface 'reschedule limit reached' even when cancel is still allowed; current implementation only shows that reason when BOTH can_reschedule and can_cancel are false, which is correct - but worth confirming UX intent.

CORS allow_origins is sourced from env (CORS_ORIGINS='*') with allow_credentials=True; per CORS spec, browsers reject wildcard with credentials. For local tests via cookies this currently works because FastAPI's CORSMiddleware echoes the origin when * is set with credentials, but it's brittle. Recommend explicit origins in production.
scheduler.py auto-complete runs every 60s; auto-cancel of stale CONFIRMED bookings is not implemented (not in scope of Phase 2 but worth noting).
Booking permissions check on cancelled bookings correctly returns can_reschedule=false, can_cancel=false, reason_blocked='This booking is cancelled.' - confirmed in tests.
test_credentials: anjali@practora.in / demo123 (pro, slug dr-anjali); raj@practora.in / demo123 (pro, slug astro-raj); admin@practora.in / admin123
seed_data_creation: Created multiple TEST_-prefixed bookings via API during pytest runs and a TEST_UI_Manage booking during frontend tests. Bookings remain in DB for inspection but are namespaced.
mocked_integrations: Resend email - RESEND_API_KEY empty, send_email() falls back to [EMAIL:CONSOLE] logging. Verified backend logs contain [EMAIL:CONSOLE] entries after booking actions.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Phase 2 booking lifecycle endpoints and UI flows are working. Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_practora_lifecycle.py and use future Monday/Tuesday/Sunday dates auto-computed from system date. Pytest takes ~5s and creates TEST_-prefixed bookings. Frontend uses cookie auth (httpOnly access_token). Magic-link URLs follow pattern /booking/{id}/manage?token=<customer_access_token>. Status flow verified: CONFIRMED -> RESCHEDULED -> CANCELLED with actor_type tracked correctly. Console emails verified via tail of /var/log/supervisor/backend.{err,out}.log.
